content.text:
13 tests, all green, and ruff is clean. Here's a summary of every change made:

---

### `src/csvstats/loader.py` — new `load_tsv` function

```python
def load_tsv(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """Read all rows from a TSV file as a list of dicts keyed by header."""
    if not path.exists():
        logger.error("tsv not found at %s", path, exc_info=True)
        raise FileNotFoundError(path)
    with path.open(newline="") as fh:
        reader = csv.DictReader(fh, delimiter="\t")
        return list(reader)
```

The implementation mirrors `load_rows` exactly — same `pathlib.Path` parameter type, same `newline=""` convention for the `csv` module, same `logger.error(..., exc_info=True)` + re-raise pattern — with the only meaningful difference being `delimiter="\t"` passed to `DictReader`.

---

### `tests/fixtures/sample.tsv` — new fixture

A tab-separated version of the existing `sample.csv` (same three rows: ada, linus, grace) so the fixture test exercises real file I/O without inventing new data.

---

### `tests/test_loader.py` — four new tests

| Test | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `test_load_tsv_returns_dicts` | Normal round-trip with `tmp_path` |
| `test_load_tsv_empty` | Header-only file → empty list |
| `test_load_tsv_missing_file` | `FileNotFoundError` raised for absent path |
| `test_load_tsv_reads_fixture` | End-to-end read of `sample.tsv` |
| `test_load_tsv_ignores_commas_in_fields` | Commas inside a field stay in one column (delimiter=tab sanity check) |

---

### `src/csvstats/loader.py` — new `load_tsv` function

```python
def load_tsv(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """Read all rows from a TSV file as a list of dicts keyed by header."""
    if not path.exists():
        logger.error("tsv not found at %s", path, exc_info=True)
        raise FileNotFoundError(path)
    with path.open(newline="") as fh:
        reader = csv.DictReader(fh, delimiter="\t")
        return list(reader)
```

The implementation mirrors `load_rows` exactly — same `pathlib.Path` parameter type, same `newline=""` convention for the `csv` module, same `logger.error(..., exc_info=True)` + re-raise pattern — with the only meaningful difference being `delimiter="\t"` passed to `DictReader`.

---

### `tests/fixtures/sample.tsv` — new fixture

A tab-separated version of the existing `sample.csv` (same three rows: ada, linus, grace) so the fixture test exercises real file I/O without inventing new data.

---

### `tests/test_loader.py` — four new tests

| Test | What it covers |
|---|---|
| `test_load_tsv_returns_dicts` | Normal round-trip with `tmp_path` |
| `test_load_tsv_empty` | Header-only file → empty list |
| `test_load_tsv_missing_file` | `FileNotFoundError` raised for absent path |
| `test_load_tsv_reads_fixture` | End-to-end read of `sample.tsv` |
| `test_load_tsv_ignores_commas_in_fields` | Commas inside a field stay in one column (delimiter=tab sanity check) |
